title: Paris World Championship
tagline: Creating a spectacle for the Riot Games World Championship using holograms.

body: This show brought together several major company priorities that all needed to shine within a single performance — the global debut of Riot's new virtual music group True Damage, a high-profile partnership with Louis Vuitton, and the celebration of the League of Legends World Championship, the pinnacle event in global esports.

Riot Games opening ceremonies had become synonymous with groundbreaking spectacle. The challenge was not only to push the boundaries creatively, but to ensure the in-venue experience felt just as special and immersive as the broadcast viewed at home.

I led the end-to-end creative and technical execution of the Opening Ceremony, from concept development through live performance. As creative lead, I directed a multidisciplinary team spanning design, animation, choreography, and live production — shaping the vision, presenting to stakeholders, and managing internal teams and external partners throughout.

My role included overseeing stage and lighting design, hologram content, and choreography, while aligning every element with Riot Games' business goals. I was on site to ensure flawless execution, managing real-time decisions and maintaining creative consistency throughout the show.
led: Oversaw stage, lighting, hologram content, and choreography
Managed internal teams and external partners on site

impact.note: The opening ceremony earned Riot Games its second Emmy Award and was viewed by more than 100 million people — going viral with 1.1B+ impressions, a 104% increase in Twitter video views, and a 221% surge in Instagram Story engagement.
